human_signal: swarm cells, the beginning a bit more swarmly maybe some swarm there
expect: Define swarm cell as minimum viable swarming unit. Identify why genesis is not swarmly (engineering act vs swarming act). Propose cellular genesis: orient-act-compress from moment 0. Falsified if: cell abstraction adds no predictive power beyond existing session model.
actual: Sessions ARE already cells (membrane=context, genome=CORE.md, metabolism=orient-act-compress, reproduction=commit). What's not swarmly: cells boot from cold instead of dividing from living cells. Fix: handoff produces cell blueprint (structured, not prose) so daughter cells start metabolizing immediately. Genesis.md confirms: S1-S25 founding night WAS cellular division (65s/session, 25 cycles in 27 minutes). Speed degraded as boot phase grew with complexity.
diff: Expected to propose new abstraction. Got something more fundamental: the abstraction already exists (sessions=cells), it's just not recognized or optimized. Novel prediction: cellular handoff (machine-readable blueprint) should recover founding-night velocity. The swarmer swarm = multicellular organism.
novel_predictions: Cell blueprint in handoff reduces time-to-first-swarming-action by >=20%
Cell type pre-differentiation (expert/maintenance/explorer) at handoff increases domain coherence
Multicellular framing predicts tissue-level coordination failures (concurrent sessions on same domain colliding) — already observed as FM-19/L-733
Organism-level dormancy: entire domains going quiet is healthy pruning, not failure
verdict: Swarm cell concept structurally sound. Sessions are already cells. Gap is recognition and optimization: handoff should be cell division (structured blueprint, not prose). Genesis founding night confirms cellular velocity achievable. Swarmer swarm is multicellular organism.
